zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：震荡偏强上行结构延续
zh.content:
当前ETH价格在1645附近窄幅整理，整体仍维持高位震荡结构。短线多头未明显衰竭，但动能有所放缓，市场进入盘整蓄势阶段。短期关注波动收敛。

技术指标方面，MACD为正值11.04，显示多头动能仍在，但信号线缺失使趋势确认略弱。价格位于SMA20之上，均线支撑有效。

下方支撑关注1621及1630区域，上方阻力集中在1650至1670区间，若放量突破将打开上行空间。关注成交量配合。

操作上建议以回调分批低吸为主，控制杠杆风险，若跌破1620需谨慎止损，多头顺势跟随。短线以波段操作为主。

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
ETH/USD is trading in a short-term consolidation phase after a mild upward drift, holding above the 20-day SMA near 1621.9. Price action remains compressed between 1639 and 1647, suggesting equilibrium between buyers and sellers. Despite limited volatility, structure still leans slightly bullish as higher lows persist and price stabilizes near recent highs. Market awaits catalyst for breakout direction with intraday momentum neutral.

Momentum indicators show a cautiously constructive bias. MACD at 11.047 suggests positive momentum continuation, although the lack of a visible signal line reading limits confirmation of strength. With RSI unavailable, price behavior becomes more important, and current tight ranges imply neither overbought nor oversold conditions. Overall momentum appears steady rather than accelerating. This supports range-bound trading with slight bullish tilt. Volatility compression reinforces consolidation behavior.

Immediate support is located at 1639–1640, with stronger structural support at the 20-day SMA around 1621. A break below could expose the 1600 psychological zone and prior consolidation base. On the upside, resistance sits at 1647.5, with a decisive breakout potentially opening the path toward 1665 and 1700. Price acceptance above resistance would confirm continuation of the short-term bullish structure. In the short term.

Trading conditions favor a range-to-breakout approach. Long positions may be considered on dips toward 1639 or ideally near the 20-day SMA, with risk managed below 1620. Breakout traders should watch for a sustained move above 1647.5 for confirmation, targeting higher extensions. Until a breakout occurs, quick mean-reversion trades remain more efficient than trend-following strategies. Patience is required as volatility remains compressed.
